differential_diagnoses: Chronic portal vein thrombosis (cavernous transformation) secondary to remote abdominal trauma
Occult malignancy (e.g., hepatocellular carcinoma or pancreatic/duodenal neoplasm causing vascular compression)
next_steps: Mesenteric angiography or CT angiography to evaluate for chronic mesenteric ischemia
Transjugular liver biopsy with hepatic venous pressure gradient (HVPG) measurement
Comprehensive thrombophilia workup (including JAK2 mutation and PNH testing)
Direct visualization of the small bowel (e.g., capsule endoscopy or enteroscopy) to rule out mesenteric ischemia or other pathology
Echocardiogram to evaluate for right-sided heart failure or constrictive pericarditis
Supportive care with titration of vasoactive medications and monitoring for mesenteric infarction

differential_diagnoses: Chronic portal vein thrombosis (cavernous transformation) with secondary portal hypertensive enteropathy
Chronic mesenteric venous thrombosis leading to mesenteric venous congestion and intestinal angina
Non-cirrhotic portal hypertension (e.g., idiopathic portal hypertension or schistosomiasis)
Intermittent small bowel obstruction due to paraumbilical hernia
Cirrhosis of uncertain etiology (possibly occult metabolic or post-traumatic atrophy)
Occult malignancy (e.g., small bowel or pancreatic neoplasm)
next_steps: Surgical consultation for elective repair of the paraumbilical hernia
CT angiography of the abdomen (mesenteric venous phase) to evaluate for mesenteric venous thrombosis or chronic ischemia
Transjugular liver biopsy with hepatic venous pressure gradient (HVPG) measurement
Comprehensive thrombophilia workup (including JAK2, PNH, and protein C/S/antithrombin)
Echocardiogram to rule out cardiac contributions to portal hypertension
Stool studies for ova and parasites (specifically S. mansoni) given the epidemiological risk
Enteroscopy or capsule endoscopy for objective assessment of bowel mucosa
